auth.label: API Token
auth.description: Pylon API token sent as a Bearer token. Generate it in the Pylon dashboard under Settings > API tokens: https://app.usepylon.com/settings/api-tokens.

domains.description: Domains for the account, without a leading scheme.

primary_domain.description: The primary domain, which must be included in domains when domains are provided.

external_id.description: The external ID. It must be unique for the object type.

label.description: The label for this external ID. It must be unique on the object.

items.description: An external ID associated with a Pylon account or contact.

external_ids.description: External IDs associated with the object.

tags.description: Tags to set on the Pylon object.

owner_id.description: The user ID of the account owner.

logo_url.description: The square .png, .jpg, or .jpeg logo URL for the account.

slug.description: The slug of the custom field.

value.description: The single value for the custom field. For select or relationship fields, use the option slug or related object ID.

values.description: The values for a multi-valued custom field, such as a multiselect field.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values to apply to the Pylon object.

inputSchema.description: Input for creating a Pylon account.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

created_at.description: The time when the account was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the account was last updated.

outputSchema.description: The created account returned by Pylon.

account_id.description: The account that this contact belongs to.

account_external_id.description: The external ID of the account that this contact belongs to.

avatar_url.description: The square .png, .jpg, or .jpeg avatar URL for the contact.

external_id.description: The external ID. It must be unique for the object type.

label.description: The label for this external ID. It must be unique on the object.

items.description: An external ID associated with a Pylon account or contact.

external_ids.description: External IDs associated with the object.

phone_numbers.description: Phone numbers for the contact. Pylon expects digits only and at most 15 digits.

primary_phone_number.description: The primary phone number, which must be in phone_numbers.

portal_role.description: The portal role slug to assign to the contact.

portal_role_id.description: The custom portal role ID to assign to the contact.

slug.description: The slug of the custom field.

value.description: The single value for the custom field. For select or relationship fields, use the option slug or related object ID.

values.description: The values for a multi-valued custom field, such as a multiselect field.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values to apply to the Pylon object.

inputSchema.description: Input for creating a Pylon contact.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

emails.description: All email addresses for the contact.

account.description: The account associated with the contact.

outputSchema.description: The created contact returned by Pylon.

actions.name: create_issue
actions.description: Create a Pylon issue with a title, HTML body, and requester or account context.

body_html.description: The HTML content of the issue body.

account_id.description: The account this issue belongs to.

requester_id.description: The contact this issue is on behalf of.

requester_email.description: The requester email address. Pylon creates a contact when none exists.

requester_name.description: The full name to use when creating a requester contact.

user_id.description: The internal user to attribute the first message to.

contact_id.description: The contact to attribute the first message to.

assignee_id.description: The user the issue should be assigned to.

team_id.description: The team the issue should be assigned to.

tags.description: Tags to set on the Pylon object.

attachment_urls.description: Publicly reachable attachment URLs for Pylon to fetch and attach.

author_unverified.description: Whether the requester's identity has not been verified.

created_at.description: The issue creation timestamp in RFC3339 format.

slug.description: The slug of the custom field.

value.description: The single value for the custom field. For select or relationship fields, use the option slug or related object ID.

values.description: The values for a multi-valued custom field, such as a multiselect field.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values to apply to the Pylon object.

destination.description: How Pylon should deliver the initial issue message.

email.description: The configured email address used to send email to the requester.

email_ccs.description: Email addresses to CC on the message.

email_bccs.description: Email addresses to BCC on the message.

chat_widget_app_id.description: The chat widget app ID used for in-app chat delivery.

from_sms_phone_number_id.description: The Telnyx phone number ID used for SMS delivery.

whatsapp_app_id.description: The WhatsApp app ID used for WhatsApp delivery.

destination_metadata.description: Delivery metadata for a newly created Pylon issue.

inputSchema.description: Input for creating a Pylon issue.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

state.description: The current issue state or custom status slug.

link.description: The link to the issue in Pylon.

created_at.description: The time when the issue was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the issue was last updated.

outputSchema.description: The created issue returned by Pylon.

actions.name: create_issue_note
actions.description: Create an internal note on a Pylon issue.

body_html.description: The HTML body of the internal note.

attachment_urls.description: Publicly reachable attachment URLs for Pylon to fetch and attach.

message_id.description: The internal note message ID to reply to.

thread_id.description: The internal thread ID to post the note to.

thread_name.description: The thread name to use if Pylon creates a new internal thread.

user_id.description: The internal user ID to post the note as.

inputSchema.description: Input for creating a Pylon internal issue note.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

id.description: The ID of the created message.

issue_id.description: The ID of the issue that received the note.

note.description: The Pylon message identifiers returned after creating a note.

outputSchema.description: The created note returned by Pylon.

actions.name: get_account
actions.description: Fetch one Pylon account by account ID or external ID.

id.description: The Pylon account ID or external ID.

inputSchema.description: Input for fetching one Pylon account.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

created_at.description: The time when the account was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the account was last updated.

actions.name: get_contact
actions.description: Fetch one Pylon contact by contact ID with optional paginated account context.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor returned by a previous Pylon request.

limit.description: The number of related accounts to fetch. Pylon requires this query value and accepts values from 1 through 999.

inputSchema.description: Input for fetching one Pylon contact.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

cursor.description: The cursor for the next page of results.

has_next_page.description: Whether another page of results is available.

anyOf.description: Cursor pagination metadata returned by Pylon.

emails.description: All email addresses for the contact.

account.description: The account associated with the contact.

actions.name: get_issue
actions.description: Fetch one Pylon issue by ID or issue number.

id.description: The Pylon issue ID or issue number.

inputSchema.description: Input for fetching one Pylon issue.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

state.description: The current issue state or custom status slug.

link.description: The link to the issue in Pylon.

created_at.description: The time when the issue was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the issue was last updated.

actions.name: get_me
actions.description: Fetch the Pylon organization associated with the API token.

inputSchema.description: Input for fetching the Pylon organization.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

organization.description: A Pylon organization returned by the /me endpoint.

actions.name: list_issue_messages
actions.description: List messages, replies, and internal notes on one Pylon issue.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor returned by a previous Pylon request.

limit.description: The number of messages to fetch. Pylon accepts values from 1 through 1000.

inputSchema.description: Input for listing Pylon issue messages.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

cursor.description: The cursor for the next page of results.

has_next_page.description: Whether another page of results is available.

anyOf.description: Cursor pagination metadata returned by Pylon.

timestamp.description: The time when the message was created.

messages.description: The issue messages returned by Pylon.

outputSchema.description: The paginated messages returned by Pylon.

actions.name: list_issues
actions.description: List Pylon issues within a required time range of up to 30 days.

start_time.description: The start time of the issue range in RFC3339 format.

end_time.description: The end time of the issue range in RFC3339 format.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor returned by a previous Pylon request.

limit.description: The number of issues to fetch. Pylon accepts 0 through 20000.

inputSchema.description: Input for listing Pylon issues. The runtime rejects ranges where start_time is after end_time or the range exceeds 30 days.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

cursor.description: The cursor for the next page of results.

has_next_page.description: Whether another page of results is available.

anyOf.description: Cursor pagination metadata returned by Pylon.

state.description: The current issue state or custom status slug.

link.description: The link to the issue in Pylon.

created_at.description: The time when the issue was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the issue was last updated.

outputSchema.description: The paginated issues returned by Pylon.

actions.name: search_accounts
actions.description: Search Pylon accounts with a filter and optional fuzzy text search.

field.description: The Pylon field to filter on.

anyOf.description: A string filter value.
A numeric filter value.
A boolean filter value.
A null filter value.

value.description: A value used in a Pylon search filter.

anyOf.description: A string filter value.
A numeric filter value.
A boolean filter value.
A null filter value.

items.description: A value used in a Pylon search filter.

values.description: Multiple values for an in-style Pylon search filter.

filters.description: Nested filters for compound and/or search filters.

search_text.description: Fuzzy text search intersected with the provided account filter.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor returned by a previous Pylon request.

limit.description: The number of accounts to fetch. Pylon accepts values from 1 through 999.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

cursor.description: The cursor for the next page of results.

has_next_page.description: Whether another page of results is available.

anyOf.description: Cursor pagination metadata returned by Pylon.

created_at.description: The time when the account was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the account was last updated.

outputSchema.description: The paginated accounts returned by Pylon.

actions.name: search_contacts
actions.description: Search Pylon contacts with a filter and optional fuzzy text search.

field.description: The Pylon field to filter on.

anyOf.description: A string filter value.
A numeric filter value.
A boolean filter value.
A null filter value.

value.description: A value used in a Pylon search filter.

anyOf.description: A string filter value.
A numeric filter value.
A boolean filter value.
A null filter value.

items.description: A value used in a Pylon search filter.

values.description: Multiple values for an in-style Pylon search filter.

filters.description: Nested filters for compound and/or search filters.

search_text.description: Fuzzy text search intersected with the provided contact filter.

cursor.description: The pagination cursor returned by a previous Pylon request.

limit.description: The number of contacts to fetch. Pylon accepts values from 1 through 999.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

cursor.description: The cursor for the next page of results.

has_next_page.description: Whether another page of results is available.

anyOf.description: Cursor pagination metadata returned by Pylon.

emails.description: All email addresses for the contact.

account.description: The account associated with the contact.

outputSchema.description: The paginated contacts returned by Pylon.

actions.name: update_issue
actions.description: Update mutable fields on one Pylon issue by ID or issue number.

id.description: The Pylon issue ID or issue number.

state.description: The new issue state or custom status slug.

account_id.description: The account this issue belongs to.

requester_id.description: The contact this issue is on behalf of.

assignee_id.description: The user to assign this issue to. Use an empty string to remove the assignee.

team_id.description: The team to assign this issue to. Use an empty string to remove the team.

customer_portal_visible.description: Whether the issue should be visible in the customer portal.

tags.description: Tags to set on the Pylon object.

slug.description: The slug of the custom field.

value.description: The single value for the custom field. For select or relationship fields, use the option slug or related object ID.

values.description: The values for a multi-valued custom field, such as a multiselect field.

custom_fields.description: Custom field values to apply to the Pylon object.

inputSchema.description: Input for updating a Pylon issue. The runtime requires at least one mutable issue field.

anyOf.description: The Pylon request ID for tracking this API call.

state.description: The current issue state or custom status slug.

link.description: The link to the issue in Pylon.

created_at.description: The time when the issue was created.

updated_at.description: The time when the issue was last updated.

outputSchema.description: The updated issue returned by Pylon.
